emails.subject: Your assessment results — Dr. Harvey noticed a few clear patterns, [FIRST_NAME]
emails.preview: What the data is actually pointing to
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME] — quick note from Dr. Harvey at Functional Medicine Florida.

I reviewed your Custom Health Assessment, and a few patterns came up right away:

* Primary focus: [CORE_ISSUE_1]
* Secondary contributors: [CORE_ISSUE_2], [CORE_ISSUE_3]
* Common symptoms in this pattern: [SYMPTOM_A], [SYMPTOM_B], [SYMPTOM_C]

Here is what 4x board certification has taught me: the most important thing is not the diagnosis — it is the root cause behind it. Most chronic symptoms have an upstream driver that standard care never identifies because the system is not designed to look for it.

The 3 things that create the most durable improvement for people with [CORE_ISSUE_1]:

1. Identify the upstream trigger — not just the presenting symptom
2. Restore the depleted system using targeted, evidence-based interventions
3. Measure and adapt — outcomes should be tracked, not assumed

I practice internal medicine, geriatrics, functional medicine, and holistic-integrative medicine in Sarasota. In-person and virtual options available.

Your next step:
* Option A: See your recommended first steps inside the app → [VIEW_MY_PLAN_LINK]
* Option B: Book a new patient consultation and let us work through your pattern together → [SCHEDULE_LINK]

— Dr. Fred Harvey, MD
Functional Medicine Florida | Sarasota, FL | functionalmedicineflorida.com

emails.subject: The upstream cause of [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM] — what standard medicine typically misses
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME],

As a physician trained in both conventional internal medicine and functional medicine, I want to explain something that changed how I practice.

Conventional medicine excels at diagnosing and managing established conditions. What it is not structured to do is identify the pre-disease dysregulation — the period where the body is clearly struggling but has not yet crossed a diagnostic threshold.

This is where most patients with patterns like yours fall. Labs are 'normal.' Symptoms are real. The explanation they receive is often inadequate.

In functional medicine, we look at the same body differently. For a pattern like yours — where [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM] is central — we are asking: what is the inflammatory burden? What is cortisol doing over the course of a day? Is gut permeability affecting systemic signaling? Are there nutritional depletions that have compounded over time?

These questions require different testing and a different kind of intake. But they produce answers — and answers produce targeted, effective plans.

That is the medicine I practice in Sarasota. Not guesswork. Not protocols. A precise map of your biology and a plan built around what it actually needs.

— Dr. Fred Harvey, MD
Functional Medicine Florida | Sarasota, FL | functionalmedicineflorida.com

[Book a New Patient Consultation →]([SCHEDULE_LINK])

emails.subject: A patient with your pattern — what changed when we found the root
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME],

One last note before I close the loop on your assessment.

A man in his early 60s came to see me after years of fatigue, cognitive slowing, and joint inflammation that had been attributed to 'aging.' He had been managed with multiple medications but felt that his quality of life was steadily declining.

His conventional workup was within normal limits for someone his age. My functional panel told a different story — chronic systemic inflammation, significant mitochondrial stress markers, and a hormonal pattern that was below the optimal range even if technically 'normal.'

We built a phased, individualized plan. Targeted supplementation. Dietary adjustments based on his specific profile. A carefully titrated hormone optimization protocol.

Eighteen months later, he described his energy and cognitive clarity as significantly improved and had been able to reduce one of his medications under his PCP's supervision.

Individual results vary. Every patient's biology and history are different. But the principle that holds across thousands of patients is: find the actual root, address it precisely, and give the body the conditions it needs to heal.

Your assessment gave us a starting map. Let us put it to use.

— Dr. Fred Harvey, MD
Functional Medicine Florida | Sarasota, FL | functionalmedicineflorida.com

Disclaimer: Individual results vary. This is not medical advice.

[Schedule Your New Patient Visit →]([SCHEDULE_LINK])

gamma_content.digital_health_report:
# 🔎 DIGITAL SEO HEALTH REPORT — functionalmedicineflorida.com
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

## 📊 DOMAIN SNAPSHOT
──────────────────

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Global Rank | #2,465,200 |
| Keywords Ranked | 351 |
| Monthly Traffic | ~237 visits |
| Traffic Value | $408/mo |
| Keywords in Top 10 | 31 |
| SERP Features Traffic | ~0 visits/mo |

---

## 🔑 THE CORE FINDING: RANKINGS EXIST — TRAFFIC DOES NOT
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The site ranks for 351 keywords yet pulls only ~237 visits per month — a conversion ratio that signals rankings are clustering in positions too low to generate clicks.

- 4 keywords at Position #1
- 5 keywords at Positions #2–3
- 22 keywords at Positions #4–10
- 19 keywords at Positions #11–20

The foundation is real: 31 keywords already on Page 1. The problem is that even those rankings are generating ~0 visits from SERP features, meaning the site is visible but not being chosen.

---

## 🤖 AI SEARCH GAP
──────────────────
Dr. Harvey's content appears in **23 AI Overview SERPs** — and is cited in **0** of them. That is a 23-citation gap: Google is surfacing AI answers in every one of those searches and pulling from competitors instead. With no `llms.txt` file and AI crawler access marked **UNKNOWN**, the site is structurally invisible to the AI layer of modern search.

---

## 🎯 BIGGEST OPPORTUNITY: FUNCTIONAL LAB TESTING CLUSTER
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
- [pos 16] functional labs — 880/mo
- [pos 7] functional lab testing — 720/mo
- [pos 17] functional blood testing — 720/mo
- [pos 15] functional laboratory — 590/mo
- [pos 7] functional medicine testing — 480/mo
- [pos 8] functional labs — 480/mo
- [pos 13] function lab testing — 260/mo

This cluster represents the highest combined search volume on the board, and the practice already has a dedicated Functional Lab Testing service page — meaning authority exists and content consolidation could move multiple rankings simultaneously.

---

## ⚡ WITHIN STRIKING DISTANCE (pos 4–10)
──────────────────────────────────────
| Keyword | Position | Monthly Volume | KD |
|---|---|---|---|
| functional lab testing | #7 | 720/mo | 22 |
| functional medicine tampa | #8 | 590/mo | 10 |
| functional medicine testing | #7 | 480/mo | 26 |
| functional labs | #8 | 480/mo | 19 |
| functional medicine tests | #6 | 170/mo | 23 |
| functional medicine labs | #8 | 170/mo | 21 |

→ **Highest priority:** 'functional medicine tampa' — 590 monthly searches, a KD of only 10, and the practice already serves Tampa patients virtually. This is the single easiest traffic gain on the board.

---

## 🏆 VS YOUR TOP COMPETITOR
──────────────────────────

| Metric | functionalmedicineflorida.com | dfwconciergemd.com |
|---|---|---|
| Keywords Ranked | 351 | 328 |
| Monthly Traffic | ~237 | ~533 |
| Traffic Value | $408/mo | $1,570/mo |

More keywords, less than half the traffic, and one-quarter the traffic value. The gap is not about ranking — it is about which keywords rank and how well the pages convert searchers into clicks.

---

## 🎯 3-PRIORITY ATTACK PLAN
──────────────────────────

**1. CONSOLIDATE THE LAB TESTING CLUSTER**
Strengthen the existing Functional Lab Testing page to target all seven cluster keywords simultaneously; moving 'functional blood testing' (720/mo, pos 17) to Page 1 alone would materially shift monthly traffic within 60–90 days.

**2. CAPTURE THE TAMPA OPPORTUNITY**
Build or expand a Tampa-specific landing page targeting 'functional medicine tampa' (590/mo, KD:10) — the lowest difficulty, highest-volume quick win currently sitting at position #8.

**3. CLOSE THE 23-CITATION AI GAP**
Install `llms.txt`, confirm AI crawler access, and restructure key service pages with clear attribution-ready content so the site earns citations inside the 23 AI Overview SERPs where it currently gets zero credit.
